evals.prompt: Design a landing page for a developer tools SaaS. Needs: hero section with animated code editor mockup, feature grid (6 features with icons), pricing table (3 tiers), testimonial carousel, and a CTA section. Use Tailwind CSS with a dark theme. Make it look distinctive — not like every other AI-generated landing page.
evals.expected_output: Production-grade HTML/React with Tailwind, distinctive design choices, responsive layout, accessibility, animations with reduced-motion support

assertions.name: distinctive_design
assertions.description: Uses creative design elements beyond generic gradients and rounded cards

assertions.name: responsive_layout
assertions.description: Layout adapts to mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints

assertions.name: tailwind_styling
assertions.description: Uses Tailwind CSS classes, not inline styles or custom CSS

assertions.name: accessibility
assertions.description: Proper semantic HTML, alt text, focus states, color contrast

assertions.name: animation_with_reduced_motion
assertions.description: Animations respect prefers-reduced-motion media query

assertions.name: all_sections_present
assertions.description: Hero, features, pricing, testimonials, and CTA all implemented

evals.prompt: Build a dashboard layout with a collapsible sidebar, header with search and user menu, main content area with a grid of stat cards, and a notification drawer that slides in from the right. Use Tailwind and make it dark-mode ready.
evals.expected_output: React component with sidebar toggle, responsive layout, dark mode classes, proper z-index layering

assertions.name: sidebar_toggle
assertions.description: Sidebar collapses/expands with animation

assertions.name: dark_mode_support
assertions.description: Uses Tailwind dark: variant classes throughout

assertions.name: notification_drawer
assertions.description: Slide-in drawer with backdrop overlay

assertions.name: responsive
assertions.description: Sidebar auto-collapses on mobile

assertions.name: stat_cards
assertions.description: Grid of cards with proper spacing and hover states

evals.prompt: Create a multi-step checkout form with progress indicator. Steps: cart review, shipping info, payment, confirmation. Each step should have smooth transitions. The design should feel premium — think Stripe's checkout experience.
evals.expected_output: Multi-step form with transitions, progress bar, premium design with attention to typography and spacing

assertions.name: four_steps
assertions.description: All 4 checkout steps implemented with distinct content

assertions.name: progress_indicator
assertions.description: Visual step indicator showing current and completed steps

assertions.name: step_transitions
assertions.description: Smooth CSS transitions between steps

assertions.name: premium_typography
assertions.description: Careful attention to font sizes, weights, and spacing

assertions.name: form_validation_states
assertions.description: Error states, disabled submit, and loading states on forms

evals.prompt: Design a data-heavy admin table page. It needs: searchable/sortable data table, filter sidebar with checkboxes and date range picker, bulk action bar that appears when rows are selected, and export button. Make it clean and functional, not flashy.
evals.expected_output: Functional admin interface prioritizing usability over aesthetics, with proper table patterns and accessible controls

assertions.name: sortable_headers
assertions.description: Column headers are clickable with sort direction indicators

assertions.name: search_input
assertions.description: Search input filters table rows

assertions.name: filter_sidebar
assertions.description: Filter panel with checkboxes and date inputs

assertions.name: bulk_actions
assertions.description: Action bar appears when rows are checkbox-selected

assertions.name: keyboard_accessible
assertions.description: Table rows and controls navigable by keyboard

evals.prompt: Build an interactive pricing page with toggle between monthly/annual billing, feature comparison table across 3 tiers, and a 'most popular' badge on the middle tier. Include a FAQ accordion at the bottom. Needs to look polished on mobile.
evals.expected_output: Pricing page with billing toggle, comparison matrix, tier highlighting, FAQ accordion, mobile-optimized

assertions.name: billing_toggle
assertions.description: Monthly/annual toggle that updates prices with annual discount

assertions.name: three_tiers
assertions.description: Three pricing tiers with distinct feature sets

assertions.name: popular_badge
assertions.description: Middle tier highlighted as most popular/recommended

assertions.name: faq_accordion
assertions.description: Expandable FAQ section with smooth open/close

assertions.name: mobile_layout
assertions.description: Tiers stack vertically on mobile with swipe or scroll
